title: astelia linearis var. novae-zelandiae
intro: Astelia linearis var. novae-zelandiae, also known as the New Zealand Astelia or Kahika, is a variety of the Astelia linearis plant, native to New Zealand, and characterized by its linear, strap-like leaves and purple or white flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and other minor ailments. The health benefits of Astelia linearis var. novae-zelandiae include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable plant in traditional Maori medicine. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties and promote health and well-being.
